analysis_scope.note: Seed 2025 contributes zero analyzable cells; seed 2024 at N=500 is invalid because evaluation rows collapse to zero.

stacked_vs_target_only_near_identity.note: Stacked is empirically almost identical to target_only on all comparable rows in this sweep.

interpretation_notes: One-third of the planned sweep (all 25 seed=2025 runs) is unanalyzable because overlap filtering left zero source rows; these are not poor scores but missing experiments.
All non-full-target N=500, seed=2024 runs are invalid because the requested calibration size consumed all 260 available target rows, leaving zero held-out evaluation rows.
Across the 20 two-seed non-full-target groups, no mean R2 exceeds the reproduced EXP-002 zero-shot baseline of 0.1070; only isolated single-seed point estimates do.
Fine-tune is the strongest source-using method relative to target_only, but its gains are modest and inconsistent; stacked is effectively indistinguishable from target_only.
